Title: Specialist root herbivore modulates plant transcriptome and downregulates defensive secondary metabolites in a brassicaceous plant

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Brassica oleracea var. gemmifera cv. Cyrus; DH1012 (mutant comparison background)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 4-hydroxyglucobrassicin-derived modified indole glucosinolates including neoglucobrassicin branch products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes hydroxylation in the secondary modification of indole glucosinolates, promoting formation of modified indole glucosinolates such as neoglucobrassicin-related products
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation by Delia radicum is associated with increased accumulation of indole glucosinolates, especially glucobrassicin and neoglucobrassicin; expression is primed by prior Plutella xylostella feeding
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly induced in primary roots after Delia radicum infestation; also elevated in roots after prior P. xylostella feeding
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Primary roots, early after root herbivory; stronger/faster induction at 15 min under Px+Dr compared with Dr alone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defensive secondary metabolites against herbivores and other attackers
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP81F4 was among the most strongly upregulated indole glucosinolate pathway genes after Delia radicum infestation. 2. Increased CYP81F4 expression coincided with higher root glucobrassicin and neoglucobrassicin concentrations. 3. Prior Plutella xylostella feeding primed faster CYP81F4 induction in roots after Delia radicum attack.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root herbivory by Delia radicum; prior leaf herbivory by Plutella xylostella or Brevicoryne brassicae
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq time course; qPCR in priming experiment; metabolite profiling of glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Brassica oleracea var. gemmifera cv. Cyrus
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering inducible indole glucosinolate responses, though defensive contribution against Delia radicum was not directly demonstrated here
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study links CYP81F4 expression tightly to induced indole glucosinolate accumulation, but functional contribution of indole glucosinolates to Delia radicum resistance was not directly tested.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: DH1012 (CRISPR knockout background); Brassica oleracea var. gemmifera cv. Cyrus (expression analyses)
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Brassica oleracea var. gemmifera cv. Cyrus; DH1012
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis; Delia radicum rapidly downregulates MYB28, and CRISPR knockout of two MYB28 copies strongly reduces aliphatic glucosinolates and increases Delia radicum emergence
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthetic genes including chain elongation and core pathway genes such as BAT5 and CYP79F2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis genes; downregulation of MYB28 is associated with reduced expression of pathway genes and lower aliphatic glucosinolate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator required for normal aliphatic glucosinolate accumulation in roots; its suppression favors herbivore performance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Three cabbage homologs were rapidly downregulated in primary roots upon Delia radicum infestation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Primary roots; downregulated within 60 min after Delia radicum feeding, with stronger downregulation in Px+Dr plants
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Root herbivory by Delia radicum; prior leaf herbivory by Plutella xylostella enhances early downregulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB28 transcripts were rapidly reduced in roots upon Delia radicum attack. 2. Aliphatic glucosinolate pathway genes and metabolites declined following MYB28 downregulation. 3. CRISPR-Cas9 knockout of two MYB28 copies (Bo2g161590 and Bo9g014610) strongly knocked down aliphatic glucosinolate production and increased Delia radicum survival from 60% to 82%.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, ENA PRJEB49273; qPCR validation; glucosinolate metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Delia radicum root herbivory; prior Plutella xylostella leaf feeding for priming analyses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq time course; qPCR; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout mutant analysis; insect performance assay; glucosinolate profiling
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced aliphatic glucosinolates in myb28 plants increase root herbivore performance; adult fly weight unchanged
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintain or enhance MYB28 activity to preserve aliphatic glucosinolate-based resistance against Delia radicum; avoid alleles that weaken root aliphatic glucosinolate production
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defensive secondary metabolites whose hydrolysis products can be toxic to herbivorous insects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is the clearest causal gene-metabolite-defense node in the paper; MYB28 was experimentally validated using gene knockout rather than expression correlation only.
